formulas.name: Balance of Trade
formulas.variables: BOT: Balance of Trade, Exports: Total exports, Imports: Total imports
formulas.notes: To calculate the difference between a country's exports and imports

formulas.name: Balance of Payments
formulas.formula: BOP = BOT + Unilateral Transfers + Capital Account
formulas.variables: BOP: Balance of Payments, BOT: Balance of Trade, Unilateral Transfers: Gifts, donations, etc., Capital Account: Investment flows
formulas.notes: To calculate the overall balance of international transactions

formulas.name: Terms of Trade
formulas.formula: TOT = (Price of Exports / Price of Imports) × 100
formulas.variables: TOT: Terms of Trade, Price of Exports: Average price of exports, Price of Imports: Average price of imports
formulas.notes: To measure the relative prices of a country's exports and imports

formulas.name: Tariff
formulas.variables: Tariff: Tax on imports, Tax: Amount of tax, Value of Imports: Total value of imports
formulas.notes: To calculate the tax imposed on imported goods

formulas.name: Quota
formulas.variables: Quota: Restricted quantity, Permitted Quantity: Allowed imports, Total Demand: Total demand for the product
formulas.notes: To limit the quantity of a particular good that can be imported

formulas.name: Exchange Rate
formulas.variables: ER: Exchange Rate, Foreign Currency: Units of foreign currency, Domestic Currency: Units of domestic currency
formulas.notes: To determine the value of one currency in terms of another

formulas.name: Foreign Exchange Reserves
formulas.variables: FER: Foreign Exchange Reserves, Total Reserves: Total foreign exchange holdings, Total Imports: Total value of imports
formulas.notes: To calculate the ability of a country to pay for imports

formulas.name: Trade Intensity Index
formulas.formula: TII = (Country's Trade with Partner / Country's Total Trade) × 100
formulas.variables: TII: Trade Intensity Index, Country's Trade with Partner: Bilateral trade, Country's Total Trade: Total trade with all countries
formulas.notes: To measure the intensity of trade between two countries

formulas.name: Revealed Comparative Advantage
formulas.formula: RCA = (Country's Exports of Product / Country's Total Exports) / (World Exports of Product / World Total Exports)
formulas.variables: RCA: Revealed Comparative Advantage, Country's Exports of Product: Exports of a specific product, Country's Total Exports: Total exports, World Exports of Product: Global exports of the product, World Total Exports: Global total exports
formulas.notes: To determine a country's comparative advantage in a particular product

formulas.name: Gini Coefficient
formulas.formula: G = (Area between Lorenz Curve and Line of Equality) / (Area between Lorenz Curve and Line of Equality + Area under Line of Equality)
formulas.variables: G: Gini Coefficient, Area between Lorenz Curve and Line of Equality: Shaded area, Area under Line of Equality: Total area
formulas.notes: To measure income inequality in international trade
